title: max platform backlog age force drain
purpose: D3.5 defines max platform backlog age force drain for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D3.5` only; siblings under `D3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: Each promotion item in `state.platform.promotion_queue` carries age; after S0 preflight the conductor compares oldest backlog age to `drain_policy.max_platform_backlog_age` from the active pack ([D5.1](D5.1-configure-task-level-params-only.md)).

When oldest backlog exceeds the cap, force-drain policy schedules platform turns until age falls below the threshold or the queue empties—even if [D3.1](D3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) K-step cadence would defer the next platform drain.

Force drain is the adversarial-review backstop across D-branch scheduling: it prevents promotion debt from rotting during long product-only sprints without overriding active product evidence gates or clearing H2 blockers.
Partial promotion with re-enqueue resets item age; marking promotion done without passing [D6](D6-index.md) checklist fails closed and reopens the queue item at H2.

If force drain would starve evidence-blocked product work, the operator tunes age and force intensity per pack, or [D3.3](D3.3-priority-cut-product-blocked-missing-tool.md) suspends drain until the missing artifact class is enqueued.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D3.5` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D3.5`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D3.5` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D3.5` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D3.5`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D3.5 defines max platform backlog age force drain for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system each promotion item in `state.platform.promotion_queue` carries age; after s0 preflight the conductor compares oldest backlog age to `drain_policy.max_platform_backlog_age` from the active pack ([d5.1](d5.1-configure-task-level-params-only.md)), then when oldest backlog exceeds the cap, force-drain policy schedules platform turns until age falls below the threshold or the queue empties—even if [d3.1](d3.1-1-platform-turn-per-k-product-turns.md) k-step cadence would defer the next platform drain.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

Each promotion item carries age; when oldest backlog exceeds max_platform_backlog_age, force drain policy schedules platform turns until age falls or queue empties—even if K-step cadence would defer drain. This prevents the improvement backlog from rotting during long project-only sprints.

Force drain is the adversarial-review backstop cited across D-branch specs. It does not override active product evidence gates; it prevents promotion debt from rotting. Age resets on partial promotion with re-enqueue; false done still fails [D6](D6-index.md) audit. Operator may tune age and force intensity per pack.
